verses.2: He has made my mouth like a sharp sword.
verses.3: He said to me, “You are my servant,
verses.4: But I said, “I have labored in vain.
verses.5: Now the LORD, he who formed me from the womb to be his servant,
verses.6: Indeed, he says, “It is too light a thing that you should be my servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob,
verses.7: The LORD, the Redeemer of Israel, and his Holy One,
verses.8: The LORD says, “I have answered you in an acceptable time.
verses.9: saying to those who are bound, ‘Come out!’;
verses.10: They shall not hunger nor thirst;
verses.11: I will make all my mountains a road,
verses.12: Behold, these shall come from afar,
verses.13: Sing, heavens, and be joyful, earth!
verses.14: But Zion said, “The LORD has forsaken me,
verses.15: “Can a woman forget her nursing child,
verses.16: Behold, I have engraved you on the palms of my hands.
verses.18: Lift up your eyes all around, and see:
verses.19: “For, as for your waste and your desolate places,
verses.20: The children of your bereavement will say in your ears,
verses.21: Then you will say in your heart, ‘Who has conceived these for me, since I have been bereaved of my children
verses.22: The Lord GOD says, “Behold, I will lift up my hand to the nations,
verses.23: Kings shall be your foster fathers,
verses.24: Shall the plunder be taken from the mighty,
verses.25: But the LORD says, “Even the captives of the mighty shall be taken away,
verses.26: I will feed those who oppress you with their own flesh;
